title: HealthChecker background goroutine and provider probing
description:
## HealthChecker Background Goroutine and Provider Probing

Implement the core health checking loop that periodically probes each configured provider.

### Files to Modify
- `internal/proxy/health.go` — the HealthChecker struct and HealthCheckerConfig already exist; implement the background check loop and probe logic

### What to Implement
- `func (hc *HealthChecker) Start(ctx context.Context)` — background goroutine that:
  - Ticks at `HealthCheckerConfig.CheckInterval` (default 30s)
  - For each configured provider, sends a lightweight probe request (HEAD or minimal POST to models endpoint)
  - Updates `ProviderHealth` fields: Healthy, LastCheckTime, LastSuccessTime/LastFailureTime, LastLatency, AvgLatencyMs (rolling average over last 10 checks), ConsecutiveFailures, TotalChecks, SuccessfulChecks, FailedChecks, LastError
  - Respects `HealthCheckerConfig.Timeout` per probe
  - Uses `context.Context` for graceful shutdown
- `func (hc *HealthChecker) Stop()` — cancel the background goroutine
- `func (hc *HealthChecker) GetHealth() []ProviderHealth` — thread-safe snapshot

### Test Command
```bash
go test -run TestHealthChecker -v ./internal/proxy/ 2>&1; echo "EXIT: $?"
```

### Pass/Fail Criteria
- Unit test: Start() with a mock HTTP server — after one tick, ProviderHealth.TotalChecks >= 1 and Healthy == true
- Unit test: failing provider (mock returns 500) — ConsecutiveFailures increments, LastError is set, Healthy == false
- Unit test: Stop() causes the goroutine to exit (no goroutine leak)
- Unit test: GetHealth() returns a snapshot (modifying the returned slice doesn't affect internal state)
- Unit test: AvgLatencyMs is computed as rolling average

title: Singleflight request deduplication in cache layer
description:
## Singleflight Request Deduplication in Cache Layer

Implement request coalescing using golang.org/x/sync/singleflight so identical concurrent requests share a single upstream call.

### Files to Modify
- `go.mod` — add `golang.org/x/sync` dependency
- `internal/proxy/cache.go` — add singleflight group and dedup logic

### What to Implement
- Add `singleflight.Group` field to `RequestCache` struct
- `func (rc *RequestCache) hashRequest(body []byte) string` — SHA256 hash of request body for dedup key (may already exist as part of cache key logic)
- `func (rc *RequestCache) GetOrDo(key string, fn func() (*models.AnthropicResponse, error)) (*models.AnthropicResponse, error)` — wraps singleflight.Do:
  - If result is already in cache, return cached (skip singleflight)
  - If not cached, use singleflight to coalesce concurrent identical requests
  - Cache the result after singleflight completes
  - Track dedup metrics: `dedupHits int64` (atomic counter for requests that reused an in-flight result)

### Test Command
```bash
go test -run TestDedup -v ./internal/proxy/ 2>&1; echo "EXIT: $?"
```

### Pass/Fail Criteria
- Unit test: 10 concurrent goroutines calling GetOrDo with the same key — fn is called exactly once
- Unit test: different keys result in separate fn calls
- Unit test: cached result returns immediately without entering singleflight
- Unit test: dedupHits counter increments for coalesced requests (should be 9 if 10 concurrent, 1 executes)
- `go mod tidy` succeeds with singleflight in go.sum

title: Fix race condition in cache metrics
description:
Location: internal/proxy/cache.go lines 35-36
Issue: hits and misses int64 fields updated without atomic operations
Fix: Use atomic.AddInt64() consistently

Current: c.hits++ (non-atomic)
Should be: atomic.AddInt64(&c.hits, 1)
close_reason: Fixed: Added sync/atomic import and changed hits/misses increments to atomic.AddInt64() and reads to atomic.LoadInt64() for thread safety

title: Refactor main.go into smaller files
description:
Location: cmd/clasp/main.go (1,686 lines)
Issue: Too large, mixing concerns (flag parsing, profile selection, subcommands)

Split into:
- cmd.go - subcommand dispatch
- flags.go - flag parsing and validation  
- profiles.go - profile management
- handlers.go - HTTP handlers initialization

This will improve maintainability and testability.
close_reason: Already completed - main.go split into flags.go, handlers.go, profiles.go

title: Fix golangci-lint formatting and code style errors
description:
## Fix golangci-lint Formatting and Code Style Errors

Fix all lint errors reported by golangci-lint so CI passes the lint stage.

### Files to Modify
- All files flagged by `golangci-lint run ./...` — expected issues:
  - gofmt formatting violations (multiple files)
  - gocritic/octalLiteral: change 0644 -> 0o644 style (7 instances, likely in file permission constants)
  - gocritic/builtinShadow: rename variables shadowing `min`/`max` builtins
  - unused: remove or use `mapBudgetToReasoningEffortWithRatio` function

### Test Command
```bash
golangci-lint run ./... 2>&1; echo "EXIT: $?"
```

### Pass/Fail Criteria
- `golangci-lint run ./...` exits 0 with no errors
- `gofmt -l .` produces no output (all files formatted)
- No remaining octalLiteral, builtinShadow, or unused warnings
- No functional changes to logic — formatting and naming only

title: Dedup integration into proxy handler and metrics exposure
description:
## Dedup Integration into Proxy Handler and Metrics Exposure

Wire the singleflight deduplication into the proxy request flow and expose dedup metrics.

### Files to Modify
- `internal/proxy/handler.go` — use GetOrDo instead of direct upstream calls for cacheable requests
- `internal/proxy/cache.go` — add metrics accessor
- `internal/proxy/server.go` — include dedup metrics in /metrics endpoint (if exists)

### What to Implement
- In the proxy handler's request flow, wrap upstream API calls with `cache.GetOrDo(hashedKey, func() { ... })` for non-streaming requests
- Streaming requests bypass dedup (they cannot be shared)
- `func (rc *RequestCache) DedupHits() int64` — returns current dedup hit count
- `func (rc *RequestCache) ResetMetrics()` — resets all counters (for testing)
- Add dedup_hits to any existing metrics/stats output

### Test Command
```bash
go test -run TestProxyDedup -v ./internal/proxy/ 2>&1; echo "EXIT: $?"
```

### Pass/Fail Criteria
- Unit test: non-streaming request through handler uses singleflight (mock upstream, verify single call for concurrent identical requests)
- Unit test: streaming request bypasses dedup (each streaming request hits upstream independently)
- Unit test: DedupHits() returns correct count after concurrent requests
- Integration: handler.go contains a call to GetOrDo (grep confirms)

title: Implement prompt caching simulation via response hashing
description:
Simulate Anthropic's cache_control behavior for non-Anthropic backends by caching full responses keyed by request hash.

## Context
Gap analysis identifies this as a missing feature — Anthropic's cache_control headers are currently stripped during translation. This feature provides equivalent cost savings when using OpenAI/OpenRouter backends.

## Deliverables

### Cache Enhancement
- Extend existing cache to detect cache_control-marked message blocks
- Hash the cacheable prefix of the conversation (system + cached user messages)
- Return cached response for identical prefixes with different suffixes
- Track cache hit savings in metrics (estimated token savings)
- Files: internal/cache/prompt_cache.go, internal/cache/prompt_cache_test.go

### Metrics
- Add prompt_cache_hits, prompt_cache_savings_tokens to /metrics endpoint
- Files: internal/metrics/cache_metrics.go

### Configuration
- CLASP_PROMPT_CACHE env var (default: false)
- CLASP_PROMPT_CACHE_MAX_SIZE for max cached prefixes
- Files: internal/config/config.go (extend existing)

## Testing
- Unit tests verifying prefix hash matching
- Test that different suffixes with same cached prefix produce cache hits
- Test cache eviction when max size exceeded
- Verify metrics accurately report savings

## Acceptance Criteria
- Requests with identical system+cached messages return cached responses
- Cache hit rate visible in /metrics
- No cache hits for requests with temperature > 0
- Graceful degradation when cache is full (LRU eviction)

title: Health dashboard HTTP endpoint and circuit breaker integration
description:
## Health Dashboard HTTP Endpoint and Circuit Breaker Integration

Wire the health checker into the HTTP server and expose a JSON dashboard endpoint.

### Files to Modify
- `internal/proxy/health.go` — add HTTP handler
- `internal/proxy/server.go` — register the /health endpoint on the mux
- `internal/proxy/health_test.go` — add integration test

### What to Implement
- `func (hc *HealthChecker) HandleHealth(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request)` — JSON handler returning:
  ```json
  {
    "status": "healthy|degraded|unhealthy",
    "providers": [ ...ProviderHealth objects... ],
    "checked_at": "ISO8601"
  }
  ```
  - "healthy" = all providers healthy; "degraded" = some unhealthy; "unhealthy" = all unhealthy
- Register handler at `GET /health` in server.go
- Include circuit breaker state in ProviderHealth.CircuitBreakerState (read from existing circuit breaker if one exists, or "none" if not implemented)
- `GET /health` must return within 100ms (it reads cached state, never probes inline)

### Test Command
```bash
go test -run TestHealthEndpoint -v ./internal/proxy/ 2>&1; echo "EXIT: $?"
```

### Pass/Fail Criteria
- Unit test: GET /health returns 200 with valid JSON containing "status" and "providers" array
- Unit test: all providers healthy -> status "healthy"
- Unit test: one provider unhealthy -> status "degraded"
- Unit test: all providers unhealthy -> status "unhealthy"
- Unit test: response includes "checked_at" ISO8601 timestamp
- Integration: server.go registers /health route (grep confirms mux.HandleFunc or equivalent)

title: Add LiteLLM backend integration for 100+ provider support
description:
Integrate LiteLLM as an optional backend to support 100+ LLM providers without custom adapter code.

## Context
Gap analysis (docs/gap-analysis.md) identifies LiteLLM as the top missing feature vs competitors (CCProxy, claude-code-proxy both support it).

## Deliverables

### LiteLLM Provider
- New provider type: "litellm" alongside openai/azure/openrouter/custom
- Proxy requests through a running LiteLLM server or embed litellm-proxy as a subprocess
- Support litellm model naming conventions (e.g., bedrock/anthropic.claude-v2)
- Files: internal/provider/litellm.go, internal/provider/litellm_test.go

### Configuration
- CLASP_LITELLM_BASE_URL env var and config.yaml support
- CLASP_LITELLM_API_KEY for authenticated LiteLLM instances
- Model mapping from Anthropic model tiers to LiteLLM model strings
- Files: internal/config/litellm.go

### Documentation
- Update README.md with LiteLLM setup instructions
- Add litellm section to clasp.example.yaml

## Testing
- Unit tests for LiteLLM provider (mock HTTP responses)
- Integration test with LiteLLM proxy server (Docker-based)
- Verify tool call translation works through LiteLLM

## Acceptance Criteria
- `clasp -provider litellm -model bedrock/anthropic.claude-v2` routes through LiteLLM
- All existing tool call translations work correctly via LiteLLM
- Streaming works end-to-end through LiteLLM proxy
- Falls back gracefully when LiteLLM server is unavailable

title: Fix TestLoadFromFileNotFound and all test failures
description:
## Fix TestLoadFromFileNotFound and All Test Failures

Fix the failing test(s) so `go test ./...` passes.

### Files to Modify
- The test file containing `TestLoadFromFileNotFound` — likely `internal/config/config_test.go` or similar
  - The test expects no error when config file is missing, but the implementation returns one
  - Either fix the test expectation or fix the implementation to match the intended behavior (config file should be optional — missing = use defaults)
- Any other test files with failures discovered by `go test ./...`

### Test Command
```bash
go test ./... 2>&1; echo "EXIT: $?"
```

### Pass/Fail Criteria
- `go test ./...` exits 0 with all tests passing
- `TestLoadFromFileNotFound` specifically passes
- No test is skipped or deleted to "fix" it — the actual behavior must be corrected
- If the implementation is changed, the change must be intentional (missing config file = use defaults)

title: Add compaction support for multi-window context management
description:
Support GPT-5.1-Codex-Max compaction for multi-million token sessions via previous_response_id chaining.

## Context
Gap analysis: GPT-5.1-Codex-Max supports compaction for extended context sessions. CLASP currently has no support for response chaining across multiple API calls.

## Deliverables

### Response ID Tracking
- Track response IDs from Responses API completions
- Map Anthropic conversation continuations to previous_response_id chains
- Store last response ID per session in memory
- Files: internal/translator/compaction.go

### Session Management
- Detect when a client is continuing the same conversation (via session headers or request patterns)
- Automatically attach previous_response_id to continuation requests
- Handle session expiry gracefully (fall back to full context)
- Files: internal/session/tracker.go, internal/session/tracker_test.go

### Configuration
- CLASP_COMPACTION env var (default: false)
- CLASP_SESSION_TIMEOUT for session tracker TTL
- Files: internal/config/config.go (extend existing)

## Testing
- Unit tests for response ID chaining logic
- Integration test: multi-turn conversation maintains context via compaction
- Test session timeout and graceful fallback
- Test with non-Responses-API models (should be no-op)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Multi-turn conversations with gpt-5.1-codex-max use compaction automatically
- Session tracker correctly identifies conversation continuations
- Compaction is no-op for Chat Completions API models
- Metrics show compaction usage vs full-context requests

title: Fix all golangci-lint and test failures in CI
description:
## Problem
CI has been red since 2026-03-11. Multiple lint and test errors:

### Lint errors (golangci-lint)
- gofmt: Multiple files not properly formatted
- gocritic/octalLiteral: Use 0o644 style (7 instances)
- gocritic/builtinShadow: shadowing min/max
- unused: func mapBudgetToReasoningEffortWithRatio is unused

### Test errors
- TestLoadFromFileNotFound: expects no error for missing config file, but gets one

### Already fixed
- go.sum missing yaml.v3 entry (55c486c)
- Duplicate TestModelAliases (cb7dfe0)

## Expected outcome
CI green on main branch.
